title: Bounded Promise Concurrency, Queueing, Ordering, Backpressure, and Cancellation · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript Pokedex interaction contains one plausible readline, token, type, validation, URL, fetch, promise, cache, abort, persistence, security, test, package, or release defect; find the first runtime or compile-time boundary failure before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded abortable detail-prefetch scheduler and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Justify prefetch from learner latency and API fair-use evidence and identify views where it must remain disabled.
Set active, queued, byte, request, time, and retry budgets independent of result count.
Track queued, active, settled, canceled, and published tasks with exactly one owner for scheduling and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 8359 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce async queue and concurrency budgets, ordering, partial results, abort, and rejection ownership; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, and packed artifact; then reorder one Promise, waiter, cache, timeout, or queue settlement and exclude stale state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Justify prefetch from learner latency and API fair-use evidence and identify views where it must remain disabled.

checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using deferred and promises plus specific case evidence.
